name: C++ Basics
description: Fundamentals of C++, object-oriented programming, pointers, templates and the STL library.
label: C++

content.text: C++ is a powerful compiled programming language that combines low-level system programming with high-level abstractions. It is widely used in game development, operating systems, embedded systems, and high-performance applications.

Every C++ program starts with the main() function. Variables must be declared with a specific type (int, float, double, char, bool). C++ uses semicolons at the end of statements and curly braces for code blocks.

Pointers are variables that store the memory address of another variable. The & operator returns a variable's address, while the * operator dereferences a pointer. References are aliases for existing variables and cannot be redirected after initialization.

C++ supports classes and objects, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, and abstraction. Classes define the properties (attributes) and behavior (methods) of objects. Constructors and destructors manage the lifecycle of objects.
